title: RNA Extraction from Brain Stem Tissue for Rabies Diagnosis by RT-PCR
author: Crystal M. Gigante
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.n4udgww

steps.title: Frozen samples should be thawed just prior to testing
steps.instruction: Frozen samples should be thawed just prior to testing. Clean the BSC work surface using RNase Away® and 70% ethanol prior to starting work. Layout plastic-lined absorbent work pad(s) in the hood to perform your work on. Place reagents, supplies, and sample in the BSC.

steps.title: Thoroughly homogenize brain pieces using a...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly homogenize brain pieces using a bead beater, micro tissue grinder or sterile applicator stick. IMPORTANT: Ensure tissue is thoroughly homogenized. Incomplete homogenization will decrease RNA yield.

steps.title: Let sit for at least 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Let sit for at least 5 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge sample at 10,000–16,000 × g for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge sample at 10,000–16,000 × g for 2 minutes

steps.title: Transfer 300 μl of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer 300 μl of the supernatant into a new sterile microcentrifuge tube. Store the remaining homogenate in a screw-top tube at -20 o C. The supernatant should be clear, pink liquid similar to TRIzol reagent. Avoid collection of lipids and solid tissue. NOTE: For very little tissue (such as from a small bat), deteriorated tissue, or non-brain tissue, use 600 μl of the supernatant for extraction.

steps.title: All centrifugation steps should be performed...
steps.instruction: All centrifugation steps should be performed at 10,000 – 16,000 × g

steps.title: Add equal volume ethanol (95–100%) to the homogenate supernatant (e
steps.instruction: Add equal volume ethanol (95–100%) to the homogenate supernatant (e.g. 300 μl ethanol to 300 μl TRIzol-supernatant) and mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Transfer 600 µL of the ethanol-supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer 600 µL of the ethanol-supernatant mixture to a Zymo-Spin™ IIC Column in a collection tube and centrifuge until the liquid has passed through the column (1 minute). Discard the flow-through. Repeat if there is more than 600 µL of ethanol-Trizol mixture for a sample.

steps.title: Transfer column to a new collection tube
steps.instruction: Transfer column to a new collection tube.

steps.title: Add 400 μl Direct-zol RNA PreWash...
steps.instruction: Add 400 μl Direct-zol RNA PreWash to the column and centrifuge for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through, return the column...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through, return the column to the collection tube, and repeat the 400 µL Direct-zol PreWash step.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and return the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and return the column to the collection tube.

steps.title: Add 700 μl RNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 700 μl RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge for 2 minutes. Ensure that the wash buffer has been completely removed. Discard the flow-through and the collection tube.

steps.title: Transfer the column carefully into an RNase-free tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the column carefully into an RNase-free tube.

steps.title: To elute RNA, add 50 μl...
steps.instruction: To elute RNA, add 50 μl of DNase/RNase-Free water directly to the column matrix.

steps.title: Incubate for 30 seconds at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 seconds at room temperature then centrifuge for 1 minute.

steps.title: RNA is now ready for immediate use or storage
steps.instruction: RNA is now ready for immediate use or storage. Keep extracted RNA on ice while in use. Carefully transfer RNA to new screw top flat bottom accession labeled microcentrifuge tube for long term storage at -70°C or colder.

steps.title: Preferably, the RNA sample should be used directly after extraction
steps.instruction: Preferably, the RNA sample should be used directly after extraction. The sample may be placed in the refrigerator for short term storage. If a sample will not be used for a few days, it should be stored under long term storage conditions at -70°C or colder. Storage at warmer temperatures or repeated freeze-thaws can lead to RNA degradation and affect diagnostic results.
